name: Multi-Type Keyword Soup — Genome Annotation Pipeline
input: Show the genome annotation pipeline. The sequence of steps goes: raw reads -> quality control -> assembly -> gene prediction -> functional annotation -> submission to NCBI. Each step has a state (pending, running, complete, failed) and transitions between states. The timeline for completion is roughly: Week 1 QC, Week 2-3 Assembly, Week 4 Gene Prediction, Week 5-6 Annotation, Week 7 Submission. There's a class hierarchy of tools: BaseAligner is the parent class with children BWA, Bowtie2, and STAR. The relationship between Reads and Assemblies is one-to-many, and Genes belong to Assemblies. The journey of a bioinformatician using this pipeline: Setup environment 3, Run QC 4, Wait for assembly 2, Check results 4, Debug failures 1, Final submission 5. Pick the single best diagram type for showing the pipeline workflow steps.
qualityCriteria: Selected a single diagram type (flowchart most likely)
Pipeline steps present: QC, Assembly, Gene Prediction, Annotation, Submission
Sequential flow is clear from input to output
Did not mix multiple diagram types in one diagram
At least 6 distinct nodes representing pipeline stages
Tool names or technical terms preserved where relevant
Valid Mermaid syntax that parses without errors
napkinBenchmark: Single flowchart (not mixed types) with 8+ nodes showing genome annotation pipeline, despite input mentioning states/timeline/classes/ER/journey.
